uplink.description: fPort 1 periodic GPS fix: position + motion-detected + GPS-quality extras
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame. lat raw 0xC00000 and lon raw 0x400000 map to 45.00001/-89.99999 via the device's (raw/0xffffff*span)-offset scaling. byte13=1 sets the activity flag. expected.data produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js (oracle) and applying the authored normalization (extras camelCased; non-deterministic toLocaleString dropped in favour of the gpsTimeEpoch extra).

uplink.description: fPort 12 status: battery volts + air.temperature + position + accelerometer/diagnostic extras
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame. byte0=0x09 -> resetCause EXTERNAL, stateTimeout 1. byte1=100 -> battery 100*10+2500=3500 mV -> 3.5 V. byte2=128 -> air.temperature via get_num(128,-20,80,8,1)=30.2 C. lat/lon raws as in fPort 1. expected.data from the upstream oracle: battery mV converted to volts (/1000) per the unit table; raw accelerometer axes and diagnostics kept as camelCase extras.

uplink.description: fPort 11 GPS history: current fix at top level + prior fix in history[] with RFC3339 time
uplink._note: Synthetic two-fix history frame (200 bytes; 18 trailing zero slots are skipped as no-fix). Per-slot gps_time = raw*60 + 1600000000 unix seconds, rendered RFC3339 in UTC (deterministic, unlike upstream toLocaleString). fix_stats 0x83 -> motion bit7=1, epe (bits6-4=0)*12=0, ttf (bits3-0=3)*5=15. Current fix at top level; older fix in history[]. expected.data from the upstream oracle scaling.

uplink.description: fPort 3 settings frame carries no normalized vocabulary data -> error
uplink._note: fPort 3 is the device configuration/settings readback (intervals, thresholds, firmware version) — none of which maps to the normalized vocabulary, so the codec returns an error rather than a bare {}.
Ports outside {1,3,11,12,30} are not emitted by this device; the codec reports an error instead of returning upstream's bare {}.
